questions.question: What does the __init__ method do in a class?
questions.explanation: __init__ is the constructor called automatically when you create a new instance of a class to set up initial attributes.

questions.question: What is inheritance in OOP?
options: A child class receiving attributes and methods from a parent class
Creating multiple instances of the same class
Storing data in a class variable
Calling a method from another object
questions.explanation: Inheritance allows a class to inherit properties and methods from another class, enabling code reuse.

questions.question: What is self in a class method?
options: A reference to the current instance/object
questions.explanation: self represents the instance itself, allowing methods to access and modify the object's attributes.

questions.question: How do you call a parent class method from a child class?
questions.explanation: super() returns a proxy object that allows you to call methods from the parent class.

questions.question: What is polymorphism in OOP?
options: Different classes responding to the same method name differently
Creating many instances of one class
Adding more methods to a class
questions.explanation: Polymorphism allows objects of different classes to respond differently to the same method call.

questions.question: What is the purpose of __str__ method?
options: Defines how the object appears when converted to string
Converts string inputs to the object
questions.explanation: __str__ returns a readable string representation of the object, used by str() and print().

questions.question: What is the difference between class variables and instance variables?
options: Class variables are shared by all instances, instance variables are unique per object
Class variables can't be modified, instance variables can
They are the same thing with different names
questions.explanation: Class variables are shared across all instances of a class, while instance variables are specific to each object.

questions.question: What does method overriding mean?
options: A child class defines its own version of a parent method
Creating multiple methods with the same name
questions.explanation: Method overriding allows a subclass to provide its own implementation of a method from the parent class.

questions.question: What is encapsulation?
options: Hiding internal object details and controlling access through methods
questions.explanation: Encapsulation protects object data by making attributes private and providing controlled access through methods.

questions.question: How do you create a private attribute in Python?
questions.explanation: Double underscore prefix (__) makes an attribute private through name mangling in Python.
